0.label: LLM Safety, Bias, and Security
0.description: Research on evaluating and mitigating safety risks, social biases, adversarial vulnerabilities, and alignment challenges in large language models.
subtopics: Adversarial attacks and jailbreaking of LLMs
Bias detection and debiasing in language models
Safety evaluation benchmarks and alignment frameworks
Robustness of AI-text detection and content integrity
LLM fairness across diverse populations and languages

1.label: Intelligent Transportation and Autonomous Driving
1.description: Research on AI-driven methods for autonomous vehicle perception, planning, traffic safety analysis, and intelligent transportation systems.
subtopics: Autonomous driving perception and planning
Traffic violation detection and enforcement
Vehicle trajectory prediction and simulation
Traffic safety analysis and crash severity modeling
Datasets and benchmarks for driving scenarios

2.label: Explainability, Causality & Trustworthy AI
2.description: Research on interpreting, explaining, and validating machine learning models through causal reasoning, explainability techniques, robustness evaluation, and reliable benchmarking.
subtopics: Neural network interpretability and explanation methods (LRP, SHAP, rule extraction, neuron-level explanations)
Causal discovery and causal inference in time series and observational data
Benchmarking and evaluation methodology for ML robustness and fairness
Counterfactual and algorithmic recourse explanations
Trustworthy AI for healthcare and personalized decision-making

3.label: Image Generation and Enhancement
3.description: Research on generative models, diffusion-based image/video synthesis, and low-level vision tasks including enhancement, restoration, and editing.
subtopics: Diffusion models for text-to-image generation
Low-light image enhancement and restoration
Video generation, outpainting, and super-resolution
Visual storytelling and narrative generation
Evaluation and training frameworks for generative models

4.label: Robot Planning, Control & Manipulation
4.description: Research on planning, control, and learning methods for robotic systems operating in complex, dynamic, and uncertain environments.
subtopics: Trajectory optimization and motion planning for robots
Deep reinforcement learning for robot locomotion and navigation
Language-guided and multimodal robot task planning
Manipulation with dexterous hands and surgical tools
Uncertainty-aware and adaptive robotic control

5.label: NLP and LLMs in Healthcare
5.description: Research applying natural language processing and large language models to clinical and biomedical tasks including summarization, reasoning, de-identification, and decision support.
subtopics: Clinical text summarization and information extraction
LLM safety, hallucination detection, and evaluation in medicine
Electronic health record representation and multimodal fusion
Medical question answering and retrieval-augmented generation
Protected health information de-identification and privacy

6.label: Efficient Neural Network Training & Inference
6.description: Research focused on improving computational efficiency, memory optimization, and compression techniques for training and deploying large-scale neural networks, particularly transformers and LLMs.
subtopics: KV-cache management and memory-efficient inference for LLMs
Model compression through pruning, quantization, and low-rank methods
Parameter-efficient fine-tuning and gradient optimization
Speculative and adaptive decoding strategies
Architectural innovations for efficient training and scaling

7.label: Optimization Algorithms and Meta-Heuristics
7.description: This cluster encompasses research on evolutionary computation, Bayesian optimization, online learning algorithms, and surrogate-assisted methods for solving complex optimization problems across diverse application domains.
subtopics: Evolutionary algorithms and genetic programming for optimization
Bayesian and surrogate-assisted black-box optimization
Online learning, bandits, and regret analysis
Integration of large language models with evolutionary computation
Multi-objective optimization and Pareto front learning

8.label: Multimodal Video and Spatial Understanding
8.description: Research on processing, understanding, and generating video and spatial information using multimodal AI models, spanning video reasoning, embodied perception, motion analysis, and edge deployment.
subtopics: Long-form and streaming video understanding with multimodal LLMs
Spatial reasoning and 3D scene reconstruction for embodied AI
Motion representation, generation, and transfer
Edge and neuromorphic AI for real-time sensor-based recognition
Video-text alignment and compositional evaluation benchmarks

9.label: LLM Reasoning and Alignment Methods
9.description: Research on improving large language model reasoning capabilities, alignment with human preferences, and computational efficiency through various training, prompting, and reinforcement learning techniques.
subtopics: Reinforcement learning for LLM alignment and optimization
Chain-of-thought and efficient reasoning frameworks
LLM benchmarking and evaluation across domains
Synthetic data and training strategies for math reasoning
Memory, metacognition, and agent architectures for LLMs

10.label: LLM-Enhanced Retrieval and Recommendation
10.description: Research on augmenting large language models with retrieval, knowledge graphs, and structured knowledge for improved question answering, information retrieval, and recommendation systems.
subtopics: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) optimization and evaluation
Knowledge graph integration with LLMs for reasoning
Sequential and context-aware recommender systems
Query understanding and natural language interfaces for structured data
Multi-hop question answering and dynamic reasoning strategies

11.label: AI for Physical Systems & Time Series
11.description: Research applying deep learning and neural network architectures to model, predict, and analyze complex physical systems, time series data, and industrial processes.
subtopics: Neural operators and PDE solving
Time series forecasting and anomaly detection
Physics-informed and spatiotemporal deep learning
Industrial process monitoring and defect detection
Domain-specific prediction applications (weather, water, energy)

12.label: 3D/4D Vision and Scene Understanding
12.description: Research advancing 3D and 4D scene perception, reconstruction, generation, and understanding through multimodal sensing, neural representations, and vision-language reasoning.
subtopics: Neural 3D/4D reconstruction and rendering (Gaussian splatting, implicit representations, stylization)
Multimodal perception and sensor fusion (RGB-D, radar-LiDAR, point clouds)
Autonomous driving and robotic scene understanding (pose estimation, localization, navigation)
Vision-language reasoning in 3D environments (open-vocabulary detection, situated QA, cross-modal retrieval)
Diffusion and generative models for visual synthesis (video generation, semantic segmentation, neural rendering)

13.label: Domain Shift and OOD Robustness
13.description: Research addressing model robustness, generalization, and detection performance under domain shifts, distribution changes, and out-of-distribution conditions across vision and learning tasks.
subtopics: Domain generalization and adaptation for object detection and segmentation
Out-of-distribution detection and robustness evaluation
Parameter-efficient fine-tuning and representation learning for generalization
Multi-domain and multi-dataset learning with annotation heterogeneity
Open-vocabulary and zero-shot model robustness under distribution shift

14.label: Multimodal Vision-Language Models Research
14.description: Research on advancing multimodal models that integrate visual and linguistic information for improved reasoning, understanding, and diverse downstream tasks.
subtopics: Vision-language model adaptation and prompt learning (CLIP fine-tuning, prompt tuning, parameter-efficient methods)
Multimodal large language model benchmarking and evaluation (reasoning, document understanding, quality assessment)
Document analysis and parsing with multimodal models (OCR, layout generation, structured document understanding)
Multimodal learning optimization (KV cache compression, modality imbalance, knowledge distillation)
Visual reasoning and out-of-distribution detection in VLMs

15.label: Privacy-Preserving Federated and Distributed Learning
15.description: Research on federated learning, differential privacy, and related techniques for training machine learning models while preserving data privacy across distributed and heterogeneous settings.
subtopics: Federated learning under non-IID and heterogeneous data distributions
Differential privacy mechanisms and personalized privacy guarantees
Memorization, membership inference, and machine unlearning in ML models
Federated learning for healthcare and sensitive domains
Knowledge distillation and aggregation strategies in federated settings

16.label: Graph Neural Networks and Learning
16.description: Research on graph neural network architectures, learning paradigms, and applications spanning dynamic graphs, self-supervised learning, molecular/protein modeling, robustness, and interpretability.
subtopics: GNN architectures and message passing improvements (over-smoothing, spectral methods, transformers)
Graph self-supervised and unsupervised representation learning
Graph-based drug discovery and molecular/protein modeling
Robustness, adversarial attacks, and trustworthy graph learning
Dynamic graphs, graph condensation, and scalable graph methods

17.label: Sign Language AI Processing
17.description: Research on computational methods for recognizing, translating, and producing sign language using deep learning, computer vision, and natural language processing techniques.
subtopics: Continuous sign language recognition architectures
Sign language translation (sign-to-text and text-to-sign)
Sign language datasets and benchmarks for diverse languages
Vision-language model adaptation for sign language
Pose estimation and skeletal modeling for sign representation

18.label: AI for Medical Image Analysis
18.description: This cluster encompasses research on developing and applying artificial intelligence and deep learning methods to analyze, segment, enhance, and interpret medical images across diverse clinical modalities and tasks.
subtopics: Medical image segmentation (polyps, nuclei, organs, occlusions)
Vision-language models and multimodal learning in pathology and radiology
Medical image enhancement, reconstruction, and synthesis (CT, X-ray, ultrasound)
Interpretable and explainable AI for clinical diagnosis
Few-shot, semi-supervised, and annotation-efficient learning for medical imaging

19.label: NLP Methods and Evaluation for LLMs
19.description: Research on natural language processing techniques spanning language model evaluation, text analysis, domain-specific applications, and interpretability across diverse tasks and languages.
subtopics: LLM evaluation benchmarks and metrics
Domain-specific and multilingual NLP applications
Text classification and sequence labeling methods
Interpretability and analysis of model outputs
Legal, educational, and creative domain NLP

20.label: Remote Sensing and Environmental AI
20.description: Deep learning and computer vision methods applied to remote sensing, environmental monitoring, agricultural systems, and domain-specific image analysis tasks.
subtopics: Satellite and hyperspectral image analysis for environmental monitoring
Agricultural automation including crop yield prediction, phenotyping, and fruit harvesting
Construction and demolition waste detection and management
Object detection and segmentation in specialized domains (smoke, marine debris, mangroves, forests)
Novel neural network architectures for remote sensing tasks (transformers, diffusion models, Mamba)

21.label: Reinforcement Learning Methods and Applications
21.description: This cluster encompasses diverse advances in reinforcement learning algorithms, frameworks, and benchmarks spanning multi-agent coordination, offline RL, exploration strategies, robustness, and real-world applicability.
subtopics: Multi-agent reinforcement learning and coordination
Offline reinforcement learning and value estimation
RL benchmarking, evaluation, and robustness to stochasticity
Reward design and exploration strategies
Scalable and adaptive RL frameworks for real-world applications

22.label: Neural Network Learning and Representations
22.description: This cluster encompasses research on improving neural network architectures, learning algorithms, and representation methods with emphasis on efficiency, interpretability, robustness, and biologically-inspired computation.
subtopics: Spiking neural networks and bio-inspired computing
Dimensionality reduction and representation learning
Model robustness, fine-tuning, and catastrophic forgetting
Interpretability and explainability in neural models
Efficient inference, compression, and optimization techniques

23.label: LLM Hallucination and Uncertainty Mitigation
23.description: Research on detecting, quantifying, and mitigating hallucinations and uncertainty in large language models and multimodal LLMs across diverse application domains.
subtopics: Hallucination detection and benchmarking in LLMs and MLLMs
Contrastive and attention-based decoding for hallucination reduction
Uncertainty quantification and calibration for LLM outputs
Fact-checking, claim verification, and evidence filtering
Sentiment analysis and opinion mining using language models

24.label: Face Analysis and Biometric Security
24.description: Research on detecting, authenticating, and analyzing facial and biometric imagery, including deepfake/forgery detection, face anti-spoofing, recognition bias, and person re-identification.
subtopics: Deepfake and AI-generated image detection
Face anti-spoofing and forgery detection
Bias, fairness, and demographic analysis in face recognition
Person re-identification across domains and clothing changes
Biometric authentication (iris, fingerprint, facial landmarks)

25.label: Adversarial Attacks and Defenses
25.description: Research on adversarial vulnerabilities, attacks, and defense mechanisms for deep learning models across various domains including image classification, language models, and security-critical systems.
subtopics: Transferable and black-box adversarial attacks
Adversarial robustness certification and training
Backdoor attacks and detection in neural networks
Watermarking and copyright protection for AI-generated content
Knowledge distillation under adversarial settings
